hormuz_closure_regime.claim: The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to commercial oil tanker traffic due to military conflict and blockade.

provenance.definition: The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to commercial oil tanker traffic due to military conflict and blockade.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Iran shut the Strait of Hormuz and mined the waterway after U.S.-Israeli strikes killed Supreme Leader Khamenei on February 28, 2026.
cases.basis_quote: Iran shut the strait and reportedly mined parts of the waterway; the U.S. subsequently imposed a naval blockade of Iranian ports.

cases.description: As of April 27, 2026, only about 7 ships transited the strait in the prior 24 hours, none of which were oil tankers carrying cargo for the global market.
cases.basis_quote: As of April 27, 2026, only about 7 ships transited the strait in the prior 24 hours, none of which were oil tankers carrying cargo for the global market.

pre_conflict_traffic_baseline.claim: Pre-conflict daily oil tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz was approximately 125-140 ships per day, with oil tankers comprising a significant portion.

provenance.definition: Pre-conflict daily oil tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz was approximately 125-140 ships per day, with oil tankers comprising a significant portion.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Pre-conflict, between 125 and 140 ships typically crossed in and out of the strait daily.
cases.basis_quote: Pre-conflict, between 125 and 140 ships typically crossed in and out of the strait daily.

us_blockade_policy.claim: The U.S. imposed a naval blockade of Iranian ports after the strait closure, preventing Iranian oil tankers from transiting.

provenance.definition: The U.S. imposed a naval blockade of Iranian ports after the strait closure, preventing Iranian oil tankers from transiting.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Iran oil tankers turned back by US blockade, Hormuz traffic sparse.
cases.basis_quote: Iran oil tankers turned back by US blockade, Hormuz traffic sparse.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: daily oil tanker transits through Strait of Hormuz reach 50 or more

provenance.definition: On any calendar day (UTC) between April 29, 2026 and June 1, 2026 inclusive, at least 50 commercial oil tankers transit the Strait of Hormuz.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: As reported by Reuters, Lloyd's List, or Bloomberg.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Days with fewer than 50 tanker transits; reports from other sources not listed.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Pre-conflict, daily oil tanker traffic was approximately 62-70 tankers per day, well above 50.
cases.basis_quote: Pre-conflict total vessel traffic was 125–140 ships/day, and normal commercial oil tanker traffic is at least 50 tanker transits per day.

cases.description: On April 27, 2026, only about 7 ships transited, none oil tankers, so far below 50.
cases.basis_quote: As of April 27, 2026, only about 7 ships transited the strait in the prior 24 hours, none of which were oil tankers carrying cargo for the global market.

reuters_lloyds_bloomberg_reporting.quorum: At least one of the three sources must report the milestone.
reuters_lloyds_bloomberg_reporting.consequence_of_nonparticipation: If none report by deadline, question resolves NO.
